title: Agent Construction Methodology
description: A methodology is a process — the HOW of constructing an AI agent or LLM-based system. Distinct from patterns (structural solutions), compositions (named combinations), and examples (instantiations). A methodology defines an ordered procedure with explicit inputs, outputs, steps, governing principles, and known failure modes. Skills (e.g. Claude Code skills) are codified methodologies and may be linked via skill_form. Pure data, no code. Single source of truth for the methodology dimension.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier. Shares the catalog-wide id namespace with patterns and compositions — must not collide. Compositions and patterns may reference a methodology by this id (validator resolves member references across all three dimensions).

aliases.description: Other names the methodology has been called in the wild.

category.description: Top-level grouping. Determines which methodologies-src/ shard the entry lives in. The enum is open to additions as new methodology families are catalogued.

summary.description: Two to four sentences. What this methodology produces, when it pays off, and what distinguishes it from adjacent approaches.

intent.description: One-sentence statement of what executing this methodology accomplishes.

when_to_apply.description: Preconditions and triggers — the situations in which a team should reach for this methodology rather than alternatives.

inputs.description: Artefacts, knowledge, or system state required before starting. Mandatory — at least one input must be named.

outputs.description: Artefacts produced by completing this methodology. Mandatory — at least one output must be named.

steps.description: Ordered procedural steps. Each step names a sub-activity and describes what happens.

produces.description: Artefacts this step produces, referenced by name.

principles.description: Governing ideas — short normative statements that the steps embody. Mandatory; at least 2 principles. A team applying the methodology should be able to point at any concrete decision and explain which principle led to it.

applies_to.description: Target system types this methodology builds. Mandatory; at least one. Open vocabulary; common values: 'agent', 'multi-agent-system', 'rag-system', 'llm-app', 'eval-harness', 'voice-agent', 'coding-agent', 'browser-agent'.

related_patterns.description: Pattern ids this methodology characteristically uses or produces. Validator confirms each resolves to a pattern in patterns-src/.

related_compositions.description: Composition ids (recipes or frameworks) this methodology produces or is typically applied to. Validator confirms each resolves to a composition in compositions-src/.

related_methodologies.description: Other methodology ids that combine, precede, succeed, or contrast with this one.

known_failure_modes.description: Anti-patterns or specific failure modes this methodology is vulnerable to when applied carelessly. Useful as a checklist during retrospectives.

pattern.description: Anti-pattern id from patterns-src/anti-patterns.json (or another pattern if the failure mode is structural).

skill_form.description: Optional pointer to a codified-skill instantiation of this methodology (Claude Code skill, ChatGPT custom GPT, agent skill, runbook). A skill is a methodology made executable.

maturity.description: Real-world standing of the methodology. 'proven' = repeatedly used in production by multiple teams; 'emerging' = stabilising, used by leading practitioners; 'experimental' = research-stage or single-team-only.

sources.description: Citations for the methodology — books, papers, blog posts, talks, repos that author or codify it. Mandatory; at least one source. Same evidence shape as compositions.schema.json.

example_scenario.description: Concrete walkthrough of applying this methodology — one to three paragraphs describing a real or representative situation: who is building what, which inputs they had, how the steps played out, what outputs they ended up with, and what they learned. Mandatory — methodologies without a worked example collapse into abstract advice.

verification_status.description: Honest grading of how well the entry is grounded in cited sources. 'verified' = every claim traces to a source with a verbatim quote; 'partial' = some claims verified; 'stub' = structural skeleton only; 'needs-verification' = was once verified, upstream may have shifted.

last_updated.description: ISO date this entry was last modified.

date_added.description: ISO date this entry was first added to the catalog. Never updated after creation.

diagram.description: Mermaid diagram source. Mandatory — every methodology must carry a process diagram showing how inputs flow through ordered steps to outputs. Rendered by the Pages workflow and natively by GitHub.

type.description: Diagram kind. Drives the expected leading Mermaid keyword: sequenceDiagram | flowchart | classDiagram | stateDiagram-v2 | graph. Process methodologies typically pick 'flow' or 'state'.

mermaid.description: Mermaid source (no fences). First non-blank line must begin with the keyword that matches `type` (e.g. 'flowchart TD' for type=flow, 'stateDiagram-v2' for type=state).

quote.description: Short verbatim excerpt (<= ~300 chars) from the cited source that supports the claim.

chapter.description: For books: chapter or section identifier (e.g. 'Ch 4', 'Part II §3.2').
